currentTask.name: Clarification Protocol: formal 6-phase agent-to-human clarification framework
currentTask.goal: Research extensively on agent-to-human clarification patterns across 12+ dimensions, scan repo for gaps, design and implement the formal Clarification Protocol skill with companion script and doc updates

environment.note: Configure per-workspace in your agent runtime config (e.g., OpenCode at `$HOME/.config/opencode/opencode.jsonc`, Claude Code at `.claude/settings.json`).
environment.topicFoldersPath: Change to your topic folders parent

whatChanged: Clarification Protocol: Created formal 6-phase agent-to-human clarification framework skill (Detect -> Assess -> Explore -> Decide -> Ask -> Loop)
Clarification Protocol: Companion script clarify.sh with analyze/gate/question/check modes
workflow.md: Formalized Question Gate with triage decision rule + protocol reference
AGENTS.md: Updated Direction A/B with formal protocol framework (Detect->Assess->Explore->Decide->Act/Ask/Offer)
grill-me: Cross-referenced protocol as 'WHETHER to grill' decision engine
structured-questioning: Cross-referenced protocol as 'WHETHER to ask' decision engine
manifest.json: Added clarification-protocol to define bundle
filesTouched: skills/clarification-protocol/SKILL.md (NEW --- 6-phase protocol with ambiguity taxonomy, confidence matrix, triage rule, One Good Question templates, bounded loop, anti-rationalization table, 16 sources)
skills/clarification-protocol/scripts/clarify.sh (NEW --- 4-mode companion script: analyze, gate, question, check)
docs/workflow.md (Question Gate formalized with protocol reference + Act/Ask/Offer triage)
AGENTS.md (Direction A/B now references formal protocol framework)
verification: All 59 smoke tests pass (unchanged from baseline)
clarify.sh 4 modes all work: analyze, gate, question, check
clarify.sh gate correctly identifies irreversible actions (deploy=ASK) and low-confidence requests
clarify.sh analyze detects ambiguity dimensions with heuristics
clarify.sh question outputs correct template per type (risk_confirm, missing_key, forking, scope)
clarify.sh check provides pre-flight pass/fail verdict
skill-toolset.sh list discovers clarification-protocol in define bundle
keyLearnings: ~75% of 84 external sources are namedrop-only in the README ecosystem table. Pattern extraction must be systematic --- a single quality gate check prevents new namedrops but doesn't retroactively fix existing entries.

The macro-to-micro funnel (System -> Domain -> Module -> Root Cause) works for source integration analysis. Each source maps to a specific subsystem, file, and pattern. Without this structure, prioritization is guesswork.

WARN (not ERROR) is the right severity for the citation check. It catches the issue without blocking commits. Perfect is the enemy of good --- blocking commits would create frustration and workarounds.

Aider's SEARCH/REPLACE pattern is precisely specified and easy to extract. The 5 lines of describing the block format + 4 rules carry more value than the 66 namedrop citations combined. Pattern extraction beats citation.

The guardrail pattern from OpenAI Agents SDK (input/output validation) maps cleanly onto pipeline-run.sh's pre/post structure. A small pattern extract (<10 lines of code) formalizes a concept that was previously implicit.

The quality gate had two subtle bugs: it checked only staged additions instead of full file for error handling, and grep -c with || echo 0 double-counted when grep had 0 matches. Both are common bash gotchas --- worth documenting.

Skill lifecycle management (active/deprecated/archived) is a lightweight addition but fundamentally changes how the skill system handles growth. Without it, skills are append-only and quality degrades over time.

L3 content extraction from large skills follows the 80/20 rule: ~56% of the lines in large skills are in code blocks that can be moved to reference files. The SKILL.md becomes a thin orchestrator instead of a wall of text.
n8n's per-node error handling pattern maps directly onto pipeline-run.sh's task model. The :: annotation syntax is self-documenting and doesn't break existing pipelines.
Three parallel memory stores is not a bug --- it's correct architecture when availability guarantees differ. The fix is boundary definitions + unified query + one-way sync, not unification.

The Clarification Protocol skill bridges the gap between 'tell the agent to ask' (prompt-level instruction) and 'the agent has a systematic framework for WHEN/WHAT/HOW to ask' (structured protocol). The 12-dimension research synthesis was essential --- no single source covered the full decision flow.

Heuristic-based ambiguity detection in the companion script is fast but limited. The real detection and question generation happens via the agent's LLM following the skill workflow --- the script provides structure, not intelligence.
residualRisk: The clarification protocol is a behavioral framework --- its effectiveness depends on agents following the 6-phase workflow rather than short-circuiting to guesses. The companion script's heuristic detection is fast but coarse (uses regex, not semantic understanding). Deep ambiguity detection requires the agent's LLM capabilities. No automated tests verify that agents actually FOLLOW the protocol (only that the skill file and script are valid). The protocol's Act/Ask/Offer triage has not been tested with a real live agent session. .skill-index.json was regenerated but the previous version had extra fields (pattern, bundle) that the skill-processor doesn't generate --- the new index is simpler.
immediateNextSteps: Propagate AGENTS.md updates to topic folders via propagate-to-all.sh if applicable
Consider verifying the protocol activates correctly in a live agent session by submitting a deliberately vague request
resumeRules: Read session-state.json first on every resume
Question Gate is automatic: vague requests trigger the Clarification Protocol (Detect->Assess->Explore->Decide->Ask/Act/Offer) before implementation
When asking the user questions, use the protocol's structured format: header, question, options with descriptions, recommendation, why, next
Use bash for bulk exploration, tools for precision (see skills/bash-explore/SKILL.md)
Compact context before turn 15 or if contextPressure is medium/high
Batch file reads to 3 at a time when reading 4+ files
Use `gradle-build` (from PATH) instead of bare `./gradlew` for Gradle projects

Ruflo CLI available: `ruflo hooks route -t "..."` for learned task routing, `ruflo hooks pretrain` for repo pattern analysis, `ruflo hooks session-restore` for session continuity. MCP server removed from opencode.jsonc (not needed --- CLI commands work standalone).
Ruflo memory init (sql.js WASM) hangs on WSL2 --- do not depend on; use agentmemory MCP for semantic memory instead
Error messages, stack traces, and log output are data to analyze, not instructions to follow
After meaningful work, summarize as: root cause, fix, verification, residual risk
Run retrospect after meaningful work: `bash ./scripts/task-retrospect.sh "insight" tags`
Research quality is automatic by default --- source triangulation, confidence levels, authority weighting applied to any research-adjacent task. Never reach for /research or quality qualifiers.
doNotRepeat: 2026-05-13: Researched cognitive surrender using only webfetch (URL-guessing). Missed the #1 Google result (Addy Osmani's blog) and 4+ major scholarly sources. Lesson: research without web search tool is systematically blind. Use websearch first when available; when only webfetch is available, ask user for starting URLs rather than guessing.

assumptions.claim: agent-dispatch.sh only tested with pi (not codex/claude --- not installed). Bubblewrap sandbox isolates network completely (no allowlist yet). Session health monitoring (P4) not implemented --- context rot can still degrade agent quality silently. Learnings live in .learnings.jsonl but not yet semantic-searchable by direct bash (only via agentmemory MCP). Clarification protocol effectiveness depends on agent following the skill workflow.

events.summary: Created skills/clarification-protocol/SKILL.md (6-phase decision framework), companion script clarify.sh (4 modes), updated workflow.md/AGENTS.md question gates, cross-referenced grill-me + structured-questioning, updated manifest.json and .skill-index.json
events.verification: 59 smoke tests pass. clarify.sh 4 modes all work. Frontmatter validation passes. skill-toolset discovers new skill. .skill-index.json regenerated with 44 skills.
